heaviest_rainfall.status: 47.89mm of rain was recorded as having fallen today, marking an exceptional precipitation event and it ranked first out of our records.  A rate of 24.6mm per hour was recorded during the 144.8 hour long storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: Today, the rain fell at an intense rate peaking at 24.6mm per hour, one of the heaviest downpours recorded and ranked 2nd from our records.  The precipitation lasted 144.8 hours and dumped 47.89mm in total.  The heaviest rain was recorded on 02 September 2025 with a peak rate of 34.3mm per hour.  The storm lasted 15.0 hours and had a total rainfall of 21.19mm.

longest_rain.status: Today's rainstorm ranked highest on our record table for the length of the storm as it lasted 144.8 hours; it delivered 47.89mm of rain at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since records began on 11 April 2025, the highest lightning strike count in one day is 23 on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: A storm is in the area.  This is the first such event since 08 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: We are in the midst of one of the longest rainy periods recorded.  This rainy period is ranked 8th since records began in 23 September 2024.  The current stretch started on 07 November 2025 and has lasted 9 days.  The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not a cold snap right now.  There have been 6 this year, compared to a usual average of 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature difference recorded was 26.7°C on 29 April 2025.  On that date the temperature reached 27.1°C and fell to 0.4°C.

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwaves.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was on 18 July 2025 and it has only happened 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (182°), which differs from the daily average south-westerly (229.0°) by approximately 47.0 degrees.    In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind gust recorded on 13 October 2012.

run.status: The top recorded wind run is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The top recorded wind speed is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 2.3°C) and drier conditions in rainfall (down 0.1 mm).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 265 m) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 140.3m higher than yesterday, 16.5m lower than the past week, 265.0m lower than the past month.

Yesterday’s cloud levels showed a low of 561m and a peak of 715m, averaging 751.9m; the week and month recorded warmer averages.

summary.summary: Inside; comfortable and pleasant temperatures. Outside; pleasant, mild temperatures. Also, air moisture should feel ok and gentle light winds are expected. A few light raindrops may fall, cloud cover will be low, possibly causing overcast or foggy conditions, and a mix of sun and clouds. The weather may change rapidly and become worse.

washing.phrase: The humidity is too high (95%) for your washing to dry quickly.  Any drying will take longer than usual.
